glossary.definition: A classification of the required level of risk reduction provided by a safety function. Higher levels indicate stricter requirements.
A potential source of harm arising from a malfunction or unintended behavior of a system.
A document, report, model, or artifact produced during the safety lifecycle to demonstrate compliance with requirements.
The ability to relate requirements, design decisions, and verification results through a documented chain of evidence.
The ability of a system to continue operating correctly in the presence of one or more faults.
